summary: This cluster focuses on the cultivation and environmental stress tolerance of brown macroalgae from the genus *Sargassum*, specifically *Sargassum fulvellum* and *Sargassum macrocarpum*, in the context of marine ecosystem management. Research demonstrated that extreme pH and salinity levels caused rapid mortality in *S. fulvellum* and its associated epifauna (*Caprella scaura* and *Grandidierella utinomi*), while a moderate range (pH 4–10, salinity 7–10 psu) preserved photosynthetic quantum yield. Concurrently, controlled manipulation of temperature and irradiance enabled accelerated maturation and artificial seed production of *S. macrocarpum*, achieving a consistent growth rate of 10.5 cm per year and one-month earlier development than natural conditions. These findings support the restoration of *Sargassum* sea forest ecosystems and highlight their potential as sources of anti-inflammatory compounds, with geographic relevance to temperate coastal cultivation systems.
